summary: Extraction fidelity is complete and non-blocking for the D+Q bone PMC full-text extraction candidate. The proposed exploratory high-p16 T3 subgroup effects, subgroup sample sizes, primary-null boundary, and adverse-event term counts match the retained PMC author-manuscript text snapshot and keep non-confirmatory limitations visible.

findings.claim_or_issue: The proposed high-p16 T3 result records preserve the full-text exploratory subgroup values: P1NP +34% at 2 weeks with P=0.035, P1NP +38% at 4 weeks with P=0.160, CTx -11% at 2 weeks with P=0.049, CTx -18% at 4 weeks with P=0.053, and radius BMD +2.7% at 20 weeks with P=0.004.
findings.why_it_matters: These are the candidate's main new quantitative extractions, and they must remain tied to the exploratory T3 subgroup rather than the overall randomized comparison.
findings.recommended_action: Retain the result values, p-values, exploratory caveats, and section:pmc-s7-exploratory-and-post-hoc-analyses provenance on the subgroup outcome and result records.

findings.claim_or_issue: The candidate records use T3 sample sizes of n=21 at 2 weeks, n=20 at 4 weeks, and n=19 for the radius BMD analysis, matching the Fig. 4 and Fig. 5 captions in the retained article markdown.
findings.why_it_matters: Subgroup denominators are small and endpoint-specific; preserving them prevents downstream users from treating the exploratory effects as full-arm n=30 results.
findings.recommended_action: Keep the result sample_size fields endpoint-specific and continue caveating limited T3 subgroup power.

findings.claim_or_issue: The adverse-event term result preserves Extended Data Table 1 counts: overall adverse events 5/30 control and 23/30 D+Q; D+Q headache 16/30, diarrhea 7/30, nausea 5/30, constipation 3/30, gastrointestinal reflux 2/30, vertigo 2/30, fatigue 4/30, body pain 5/30; and control body pain 2/30.
findings.why_it_matters: The source table has blank cells for several control event terms, so extraction must not silently convert missing table cells into explicit zero-event counts.
findings.recommended_action: Retain the descriptive group_values extraction and the caveat that blank table cells are not encoded as zeros or used for pooled safety effects.

findings.claim_or_issue: The updated finding separates the overall null primary 20-week CTx endpoint from secondary P1NP signals and exploratory high-p16 T3 subgroup signals.
findings.why_it_matters: The extraction lane can accept the candidate only if the source-backed subgroup effects are not promoted into an overall efficacy claim.
findings.recommended_action: Keep the finding caveats and synthesis boundaries that prevent exploratory subgroup records from standing in for the overall randomized endpoint.
